data.title: Asia equity markets subdued; investors weigh fragile peace talks and regional earnings results
data.description:
Asian equity markets were mostly lower as market sentiment turned cautious ahead of corporate earnings and amid uncertainty over a US-Iran ceasefire extension and peace talks. 

Gold prices rose above $4,750 an ounce on Wednesday.

WTI crude futures stayed above $89 per barrel on Wednesday after rising more than 2% in the previous session.

The benchmark KOSPI fell 0.9% to around 6,330 on Wednesday. The South Korean won firmed to around 1,480 per dollar.

Japan (NKY:IND) fell 0.15% to above 59,400, while the broader Topix Index fell 0.8% to 3,740 on Wednesday. The Japanese yen traded around 159.2 per dollar on Wednesday after declining for two consecutive sessions.

Japan’s trade surplus reached JPY 667.0 billion in March 2026, an increase from JPY 529.8 billion last year but below the expected JPY 1,106 billion. Exports rose 11.7% to JPY 11,003.3 billion, and imports climbed 10.9% to JPY 10,336.3 billion due to strong domestic demand from a stimulus program.

China's (SHCOMP) rose 0.26% to around 4,089 on Wednesday, hitting a fresh one-month high, while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.4% to 15,040, its strongest level since December 2021, and the offshore yuan 

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 1.47% to 26,180 on Wednesday, snapping a two-session winning streak as investors turned cautious amid a mixed global backdrop. 

India (SENSEX) fell 0.95% to 78,659 on Wednesday, halting a three-day winning streak and pulling back from a recent six-week high.

Moody’s Ratings slashed India’s FY2027 growth forecast to 6% from 6.8% earlier, citing weaker private consumption amid higher energy prices due to the ongoing Middle East conflict. 

Australia (AS51) fell 1.19% to 8,865 in Wednesday morning trade, extending a run of weak sessions. The Australian dollar held its recent decline to below $0.72.

The Westpac–Melbourne Institute Leading Economic Index inched down 0.1% month-on-month in March 2026.

In the U.S. on Monday, all three major indexes ended lower as investors worried a US-Iran peace deal would not be reached before the ceasefire expires Wednesday

U.S. stock futures advanced on Wednesday after President Donald Trump extended the current ceasefire with Iran: Dow +0.53%; S&P 500 +0.58%; Nasdaq +0.74%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia's markets and economy:

data.title: United Airlines in charts: Q1 unit revenues hit multi-year highs as PRASM and TRASM rise Y/Y
Tron founder Justin Sun sues Trump-linked WLFI over token rights
data.description:
Crypto billionaire Justin Sun has filed a lawsuit against U.S. President Donald Trump's World Liberty Financial, escalating a high-profile dispute over control and governance of the project’s WLFI token.

Sun, one of the largest outside investors in WLFI, filed the case in a California federal court, claiming the company violated his rights as a token holder. He alleges that WLFI wrongfully froze his entire WLFI holdings, preventing transfers or use.

The billionaire Tron founder claims the platform secretly embedded a “blacklist” or freeze function in the WLFI token smart contract without consent, giving executives unilateral power to lock user wallets without clear on‑chain disclosure.

He says his own wallet was blacklisted in September 2025 after he began moving large amounts of WLFI, effectively stripping him of voting and liquidity rights. WLFI has denied Sun’s accusations, calling them “baseless” and asserting that freezing his wallet was justified by alleged misconduct or breaches of his investment terms.

"I have tried in good faith to resolve this situation with the World Liberty project team without resorting to litigation. But the project team has refused my requests to unfreeze my tokens and restore my rights as a token holder. They have left me with no choice but to turn to the courts," Justin Sun said in a post on social media platform X.

The lawsuit follows days of escalating tensions between Sun and the Trump-linked crypto venture.

World Liberty Financial is a decentralized finance venture launched in 2024 with backing from Donald Trump and his family. The WLFI token is marketed as a governance asset, giving holders voting rights similar to shareholders.

data.title: FDA expands Sanofi’s Tzield use to children as young as 1 for type 1 diabetes delay
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved a supplemental biologics license application for Sanofi’s (SNY) Tzield (teplizumab-mzwv), expanding its use to patients as young as one year old to delay the onset of stage 3 type 1 diabetes in those diagnosed with stage 2 disease.

The approval was granted under a priority review process and is supported by one-year data from the PETITE-T1D phase 4 study, evaluating safety and pharmacokinetics in young children, the company said.

Tzield is also being reviewed by the FDA for a potential indication to delay the progression of stage 3 T1D in patients eight years of age and older recently diagnosed with stage 3 T1D.

Tzield is approved in the EU (under the name Teizeild), the UK, China, Canada, Israel, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Kuwait, and Brazil to delay the onset of stage 3 T1D in adults and pediatric patients eight years and older diagnosed with stage 2 T1D. Other regulatory reviews are ongoing.

Tzield was previously granted FDA breakthrough therapy designation and orphan drug designation for medicines that treat rare diseases affecting fewer than 200,000 people in the US, Sanofi (SNY) added.

data.title: Netflix eyes ‘Seinfeld’ studio lot at deep discount after dropping WBD bid: report
data.description:
• Netflix (NFLX) dropped out of the bidding for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), but it may now get another, smaller studio lot for a lot less money, according to a Bloomberg News report.
  • The streaming TV pioneer is said to be in talks to buy a historic Los Angeles movie studio lot for a fraction of its 2021 $1.85B sale price after lenders including Goldman Sachs Group (GS) repossessed the property, the report said.
  • The final price for Radford Studio Center, a one-time silent movie lot where TV series such as Gilligan’s Island and Seinfeld were shot, hasn’t been determined, and the deal still hasn’t closed, according to three people with knowledge of the discussions.
  • The current owner, Hackman Capital Partners, defaulted on $1.1B of debt and turned the property over to lenders led by Goldman after it was unable to reach a refinancing deal last year.
  • Netflix (NFLX), which has historically leased rather than owned real estate, has stepped up investing in studio lots. It’s currently developing a $1B studio lot in Fort Monmouth, New Jersey.

data.title: Japan posts second straight trade surplus in March but misses ¥1.1T forecast; exports hit record high
Convenience store Yesway prices $280M IPO at lower range of $20 per share
data.description:
• Convenience store operator Yesway has raised $280M in a U.S. IPO, selling 14M shares at $20 each, pricing at the lower end of its marketed range of $20 to $23.
  • Founded in 2015 by Brookwood Financial Partners, Yesway (YSWY) operates more than 400 convenience stores across nine U.S. states.
  • The Fort Worth, Texas-based company has an implied valuation of about $1.21B.
  • The shares are expected to begin trading on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "YSWY" on April 22, 2026.
  • The offering is expected to close on April 23, 2026.
  • Banks, including Morgan Stanley, J.P. Morgan, and Goldman Sachs are acting as bookrunners.
  • Yesway (YSWY) had initially pursued an IPO as early as 2021 but delayed plans in 2022 due to volatile market conditions.

data.title: Musk's SpaceX weighs $60B buyout of Cursor or $10B partnership payout
data.description:
SpaceX (SPACE) Tuesday revealed that Cursor has granted the company an option to acquire the firm later this year in a deal valued at $60B, or alternatively pay $10B tied to their joint work.

"The combination of Cursor’s leading product and distribution to expert software engineers with SpaceX’s million H100 equivalent Colossus training supercomputer will allow us to build the world’s most useful models," Elon ​Musk's rocket maker said in a post on X.

"SpaceXAI and @cursor_ai are now working closely together to create the world’s best coding and knowledge work AI," the post said.

The move follows reports that xAI (X.AI), which is now a part of SpaceX (SPACE), may rent computing capacity to AI startup Cursor amid soaring demand.

SpaceX (SPACE) experienced a wave of developments this week as it pushes ahead toward what could become the largest initial public offering ever. The rocket and spacecraft manufacturer is targeting a listing valuation of roughly $1.75T with a $75B raise.

Meanwhile, Cursor is reportedly in talks to raise a $2B fundraising round at an over $50B valuation.

Last year in November, Cursor raised about $2.3B at a $29.3B post-money valuation in a Series D funding round, which included existing investors, including Accel, Thrive, and Andreessen Horowitz, and new partners Coatue, Nvidia (NVDA), and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google.
